• Per the 12 Apr 2026 rep note from Dan Quinn: 'RFP clarifications received, (a lot) deadline is Fri 24th April - some updated eProc/Intake Merlin user numbers and another requirement to outline what modules are related to each of the ~500 requirements.' The buyer is asking specifically for updated Merlin user numbers, which signals Merlin/Intake is in scope and being sized - but it's commercial sizing, not an AI vision statement.
• Per the 18 Feb 2026 rep note: 'RFP received today... looks like: iAnalyze, iSupplier, CLM, iSource maybe iRisk & Intake) - deadline of 28/2 will be tight.' The RFP product mention is functional (iAnalyze/iSupplier/CLM/Intake), not 'AI Needs / Agentic / autonomous sourcing.' • Medium confidence: Products in scope include Merlin (and rep notes mention Intake Merlin user counts), NextStep is dense across ~10 dated entries, but Avoma fuzzy-matched to a different account (SwWDuQA - no recordings) so no buyer transcript. RFP-driven cycle without explicit AI evaluation criterion quoted from a named buyer.

metrics.narrative: Tushar Patel articulated qualitative pain on the 11 December 2025 discovery call: 'The main purpose is to streamline processes and build a more robust and transparent system' (speaker_id 1, 11 Dec 2025). The buyer is moving from manual procurement and contract processes to a digital S2P environment, seeking transparency, productivity, and efficiency gains across multiple regions (MEDDPICC entry 11 Feb 2026). No quantified ROI, cost savings, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement has been discussed. Tushar declined the Zycus business-case development service on the 11 Feb 2026 call, stating he would 'talk to CFO' but no follow-up confirming engagement with that service. The gap: no quantified value case anchoring the $175k investment. Next move: deliver a concise ROI model tying Zycus to Arabian Industries' stated pain (manual process risk, opacity, inefficiency) with benchmark savings percentages, and secure CFO validation of the business case upon restart in July/August 2026.

champion.narrative: Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist) is the identified champion. He initiated the discovery call (11 Dec 2025), led the RFP process (Feb-March 2026), confirmed the shortlist on 22 June 2026, and has been the sole point of contact throughout. The MEDDPICC entry (11 Feb 2026) states 'Tushar Patel has potential to be champion / internal driver: leading discovery, scanning market, defining scope, and running RFP.' Evidence of advocacy: Tushar committed on 11 Feb 2026 to arrange a CFO and MD pitch after the follow-up session ('He has agreed that we can meet/pitch Zycus to CFO (+maybe MD) after the next session' — SF Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026), and he confirmed on 22 June 2026 that the project will restart in July/August with deeper demos and commercial negotiations (SF Next_Step__c 22 June 2026), signaling he is maintaining momentum despite the exec pause. However, Tushar is mid-level (Procurement Specialist, not Head of Procurement or Supply Chain), and he has not delivered on the CFO/MD introduction (4+ months since the 11 Feb commitment). His engagement is active (last call 22 June, email 17 June), but his influence is unproven — he cannot approve budget or override the exec pause. The gap: Tushar's access to the Economic Buyer (CFO) and Decision Maker (MD) is unverified, and we have no evidence he is advocating for Zycus vs peers (he has not shared competitive intel, nor pushed back on GEP/Ariba/iValua). Champion strength: developing. He is engaged and driving the process, but he has not yet opened the door to power or demonstrated the ability to navigate the exec team. At risk: if the CFO or MD deprioritize S2P upon restart, or if a peer vendor secures exec engagement first, Tushar may be overruled. Next move: upon restart, ask Tushar directly to arrange the CFO and MD call within the first two weeks of July, framing it as a 15-minute exec-level value briefing (transparency, risk, efficiency) ahead of deeper demos, and use that call to assess whether Tushar can deliver access and whether the CFO is genuinely sponsoring this project.

competition.narrative: Competition is confirmed but visibility is limited. The MEDDPICC entry (11 Feb 2026) lists the assumed shortlist: 'Not clear yet - assume Ariba, iValua, GEP ...maybe not Coupa due to scope?' Salesforce Competitors__c field names five: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba. Tushar confirmed on 22 June 2026 that Zycus is shortlisted (top 3), which narrows the field but he did not name the other two finalists (SF Next_Step__c 22 June 2026). No buyer commentary on competitor positioning, pricing, or preference has surfaced in the calls. The strongest current threat is GEP Worldwide: GEP is a known S2P peer in the APAC/Middle East market, and the fact that Coupa was questioned ('maybe not Coupa due to scope?') while GEP was consistently assumed suggests GEP is the best-fit rival. SAP Ariba and iValua are also credible enterprise S2P players. JAGGAER and Coupa are less certain (Coupa may be out due to scope/fit or pricing). The wedge: Zycus's Merlin agentic AI (ANA) for sourcing and negotiation, and the Epicor integration confirmed on 11 Feb 2026. Tushar expressed strong interest in agentic AI on the 11 Dec 2025 discovery call ('interested in Supplier Mgt, Sourcing and Agentic AI (mostly ANA)' — SF Next_Step__c 11 Dec 2025), and the 11 Feb 2026 demo included AI-driven negotiation and autonomous supplier awarding (Avoma 11 Feb 2026). The gap: no intel on how GEP, Ariba, or iValua are positioning their AI or pricing, and no evidence of buyer preference (Tushar has not shared scoring, nor indicated which vendor is leading). The risk: during the 4+ month pause (Feb-June 2026), peers may have secured exec engagement or delivered business cases that position them ahead post-restart. Next move: upon restart, run a competitive review with Tushar — ask him to share his preliminary scoring of Zycus vs the other two finalists on the key criteria (Epicor integration, AI/ANA, sourcing, phased rollout), surface any gaps or concerns, and position Merlin as the differentiated Phase 1 anchor (agentic negotiation on supplier pricing, not just generative summaries).

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated. On the 11 Dec 2025 discovery call, Tushar Patel stated: 'The main purpose is to streamline processes and build a more robust and transparent system' (Avoma 11 Dec 2025, speaker_id 1). The MEDDPICC entry (11 Feb 2026) expands: Arabian Industries is moving from manual procurement and contract processes to a digital, robust, transparent S2P environment; they seek to create transparency and control in procurement (described as a 'gray area'), reducing risk of opacity and inefficiency, and increasing productivity and efficiency across multiple regions. The pain owner is Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist), who is leading the evaluation, but the CFO and MD are the ultimate sponsors (they must approve the investment to solve this pain). The pain is confirmed qualitatively; the gap is quantification (no cost of manual process, error rate, or cycle-time baseline stated). The buyer declined the Zycus business-case service on 11 Feb 2026, so the quantified pain case has not been built. Next move: upon restart, tie the qualitative pain to a concise quantified narrative (e.g. procurement cycle time reduction, supplier onboarding time, contract risk exposure) using industry benchmarks, and validate it with the CFO to anchor the $175k investment.

economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO (Mahesh Nair) is the identified Economic Buyer, making the decision 'in collaboration with our MD' (MEDDPICC entry 11 Feb 2026). Tushar Patel committed on the 11 Feb 2026 call to arrange a high-level overview call with the CFO and MD after the follow-up Spend Analysis session, stating 'He has agreed that we can meet/pitch Zycus to CFO (+maybe MD) after the next session' (SF Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026). However, that follow-up session was never scheduled, and as of 22 June 2026 the CFO and MD have not been engaged. The CFO owns the budget but has not validated the $175k investment or confirmed budget availability (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026: 'No budget assigned yet; they are still figuring it out'). The EB is mapped but passive; no two-way conversation with the CFO has occurred, no exec-level value narrative delivered, and no sponsorship secured. The gap: the CFO has not bought in, and with the exec team pausing non-essential projects due to the war, there is no evidence the CFO will prioritize S2P upon restart. Next move: upon restart in July/August, the deal owner's manager should engage the CFO directly with a concise value narrative (transparency, risk reduction, procurement efficiency) and validate budget authority and investment appetite before deeper demos resume.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is structured and visible. Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist) is leading the evaluation: he ran a one-month discovery/market scan (December 2025), shortlisted 4-5 suitable products, launched an official RFP (18 Feb 2026), collected responses (deadline extended to 30 March 2026), and confirmed the shortlist to top 3 on 22 June 2026 (SF Next_Step__c). The process: Tushar will run deeper demonstrations and gap analysis, then present his recommendation to the CFO; the CFO and MD will make the final decision (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026). Approval chain: the CFO must approve budget, and the MD (Managing Director) is involved in the final decision 'in collaboration with' the CFO (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026). The timeline: decision was originally targeted for Q2 (April/May 2026, stated 11 Feb 2026), but slipped to Q3 2026 due to the exec team pausing all non-essential projects because of the regional war; restart expected July/August 2026 with deeper demos and commercial negotiations (SF Next_Step__c 22 June 2026). The process is on hold, externally imposed, not buyer disengagement. The gap: no visibility into whether the CFO or MD will re-prioritize S2P upon restart, and no governance/steering committee mentioned — Tushar appears to be running the evaluation alone without a cross-functional team, which may signal lower exec urgency. Next move: upon restart, secure a governance structure (CFO sign-off on timeline and budget, multi-department stakeholder map) and validate that S2P remains a priority vs other initiatives that may have escalated during the pause.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are well-documented from the discovery and demo calls, and formalized in the RFP process. Tushar stated on 11 Dec 2025 that the solution 'must integrate as an add-on to Epicor, not replace ERP' (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026). On the 11 Feb 2026 demo, Tushar confirmed 'Integration with Epicor was confirmed and seamless connectivity assured' (Avoma notes 11 Feb 2026). The buyer's stated requirements: strong vendor onboarding and supplier management (complete online process), sourcing (RFX/RFP with automated comparison and e-Auctions), AI capabilities (agentic and generative) supporting decision-making in sourcing and vendor selection, and phased, non-disruptive rollout to avoid change-management resistance (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026). The RFP issued 18 Feb 2026 included approximately 500 requirements across iAnalyze, iSupplier, CLM, iSource, iRisk, and Intake modules, with clarifications exchanged through April 2026 (SF Next_Step__c). Zycus submitted the RFP response 30 March 2026, positioning it as a budgetary estimate due to missing metrics (SF Next_Step__c 30 March 2026). The criteria are confirmed; the gap is that Zycus has not yet delivered the deeper use-case demonstrations promised post-RFP (delayed by the exec pause), and we do not know how GEP, Ariba, iValua, or JAGGAER are positioned against these criteria. Next move: upon restart, run the deeper demonstration focusing on the Epicor integration, AI-driven sourcing (ANA), and phased rollout story, and secure Tushar's scoring of Zycus vs peers on each criterion.

RFP-driven cycle without explicit AI evaluation criterion quoted from a named buyer.

deltas.to: Secure CFO and MD engagement before competitors do. The deal is single-threaded on Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist, mid-level) with no relationships to the
deltas.subject: Secure CFO and MD engagement before competitors do. The deal is single-threaded on Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist, mid-level) with no relationships to the CFO (Mahesh Nair, economic buyer and budget owner) or MD (decision co-sponsor). Tushar committed on 2026-02-11 to arrange a CFO/MD pitch after the follow-up Spend Analysis session, but that session has not occurred and no exec introduction has been made. The exec team paused all non-essential projects on 2026-06-22 due to regional war, and restart is contingent on exec re-prioritization. If GEP or Ariba secures CFO/MD engagement first during the pause or upon restart, they will control the narrative. The highest-leverage move is to bypass the pending follow-up session and request a direct CFO/MD business-case discussion now, positioning Zycus as the strategic AI-led S2P partner before the restart.

deltas.to: Deliver a quantified business case to the CFO before the restart. The buyer has articulated qualitative pain (manual processes, lack of transparency, inefficien
deltas.subject: Deliver a quantified business case to the CFO before the restart. The buyer has articulated qualitative pain (manual processes, lack of transparency, inefficiency) but no quantified ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been stated. The MEDDPICC entry from 2026-02-11 notes 'No budget assigned yet; they are still figuring it out.' On the 2026-02-11 demo, Zycus offered a business-case development service to quantify potential value, but Tushar has not confirmed engagement. Without a quantified value case, the CFO cannot defend the $176k investment against other post-war priorities. Competitors may deliver a business case during the pause. The move is to proactively build and present a quantified ROI model (cost savings, productivity gains, risk reduction) to the CFO before the July/August restart, anchoring Zycus as the value leader.

deltas.to: Multi-thread beyond Tushar Patel to de-risk single point of failure. The deal has only 1 buyer-side contact role (Tushar Patel, Procurement Specialist). There a
deltas.subject: Multi-thread beyond Tushar Patel to de-risk single point of failure. The deal has only 1 buyer-side contact role (Tushar Patel, Procurement Specialist). There are no relationships with the CFO (Mahesh Nair), MD, Head of Supply Chain (Shiva Prakash), or Head of Contracting/Sourcing (Khalfan Al Mabsali). If Tushar loses priority post-restart, changes role, or is overruled by the exec team, the deal is at risk. The account has 16 procurement/supply-chain contacts in Salesforce but none are engaged on this opportunity. The move is to request Tushar introduce Zycus to the Head of Supply Chain (Shiva Prakash) and Head of Contracting (Khalfan Al Mabsali) as part of the post-restart deeper demos, positioning it as a cross-functional S2P transformation (not just procurement tooling) to build a multi-threaded coalition.
